name: Orbit Notes
oneLineDescription: A lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items
whatItIs: Orbit Notes is a lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items. It helps small teams turn messy conversations into clear next steps. The primary use case is a team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates, and everyone can later search, filter, and track progress.
coreUserFlows: A team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates.
Team members search, filter, and track progress on action items.
Users sign up creating a new team or join an existing team via invite.
Team Leads manage team membership, roles, and team identity.

dataModel.purpose: Stores user account information with team membership and role
Stores team information and serves as data scoping boundary
Containers for organizing notes within a team
Core content units capturing meeting discussions and decisions
Tasks extracted from notes that can be assigned and tracked

crossCuttingPrinciples: All data access is scoped to the user's team with no cross-team leakage.
Three-tier authorization model with Viewer, Contributor, and Team Lead roles with increasing permissions.
A team must always have at least one Team Lead.
Users cannot exist without belonging to a team.
specSummary: Orbit Notes is a team workspace designed to help small teams capture meeting notes, track decisions, and manage action items. The application uses a single-team model where each user belongs to exactly one team and all data is scoped accordingly. The core workflow centers around creating notes within projects, extracting actionable tasks, and assigning them to team members for tracking. The system implements a three-tier authorization model with Viewers having read-only access, Contributors able to create and edit content, and Team Leads having full administrative capabilities including team management and project oversight. Key features include rich text note editing with basic formatting, project-based organization, action item assignment and completion tracking, keyword search across all team notes, and an activity timeline showing recent team activity. The technical foundation uses Next.js 14 with App Router for the frontend, Supabase for authentication and PostgreSQL database backend, and Tailwind CSS for styling. The application deliberately excludes multi-team functionality, file attachments, real-time collaborative editing, notifications, and mobile native apps in favor of a focused, lightweight experience optimized for single-team collaboration and meeting follow-up workflows.
